# IAA Pre-Brief — Wave mmm-39b-frs-derivation-fix

---

## Wave Summary

This wave makes a surgical fix to `modules/MMM/00-app-description/MMM_app_description.md`
Section 39B (Requirements Derivation Chain). The current text states:

> "FRS derives functional requirements from the App Description"

The required corrected text is:

> "FRS derives functional requirements from the App Description and the UX Workflow & Wiring Spec (Stage 2)"

Additionally, the document version header must be bumped from v0.3.0 → v0.4.0 with an
updated Last Updated date (2026-04-07).

**No other changes are permitted.** No production code, no schema, no CI changes, no other
sections of the App Description.

**Canonically grounded**: This fix aligns Section 39B with `PRE_BUILD_STAGE_MODEL_CANON.md`
§Stage 3 (FRS), which explicitly states:
> "Purpose: Convert the approved App Description and UX Workflow & Wiring Spec into explicit,
> traceable functional requirements."

**Delegated Agent**: governance-liaison-isms-agent (or equivalent doc-governance agent
designated by Foreman)

---

## ⚠️ IAA CATEGORY CORRECTION

> The invoking request classified this wave as `KNOWLEDGE_GOVERNANCE`.
> IAA's independent classification supersedes the invoking classification.

**Correct Category**: `PRE_BUILD_STAGE_MODEL`

**Trigger path**: `modules/*/00-app-description/` is a hard trigger for `PRE_BUILD_STAGE_MODEL`
per `iaa-trigger-table.md` §Decision Flow Step 7. The file `modules/MMM/00-app-description/MMM_app_description.md`
matches this path exactly. The AMBIGUITY RULE does not apply — the trigger is unambiguous.

**KNOWLEDGE_GOVERNANCE does NOT apply**: This file is not a Tier 2 knowledge file, not in
`.agent-workspace/*/knowledge/`, and is not an IAA operational registry. It is a module
App Description governance artifact under the pre-build lifecycle model.

## FFA (Full Forensic Audit) Checks

**FFA does NOT apply to this wave.**

Full Forensic Audit (FFA) checks are defined in the `BUILD_DELIVERABLE` overlay and are
triggered exclusively by `BUILD_DELIVERABLE` / `AAWP_MAT` category PRs (production code,
schema, API, UI implementation). This wave is `PRE_BUILD_STAGE_MODEL` — a doc-governance
correction. No FFA sweep is required.

**What does apply instead**: PRE_BUILD_GATES overlay (OVL-PBG-001 through OVL-PBG-009) —
see §Applicable Checks below.

---

## Applicable Checks at Final Assurance Invocation

When the builder completes work and CodexAdvisor invokes IAA for the final assurance verdict,
IAA will execute:

### Core Invariants (all apply — CORE-001 through CORE-023)

All universal core invariants apply. Key ones for this wave:

| Check | Relevance |
|-------|-----------|
| CORE-007 | No placeholder content in delivered artifacts |
| CORE-013 | IAA invocation evidence present |
| CORE-015 | Session memory present |
| CORE-016 | IAA token file exists (§4.3b architecture) |
| CORE-017 | No `.github/agents/` modifications by unauthorized agent |
| CORE-018 | Complete evidence artifact sweep (PREHANDOVER + session memory + token) |
| CORE-021 | Zero-severity-tolerance — any finding = REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| CORE-023 | Workflow integrity ripple check (N/A expected — no workflow-adjacent files) |

### PRE_BUILD_GATES Overlay (OVL-PBG-001 through OVL-PBG-009)

| Check ID | Check Name | What IAA Will Verify |
|----------|-----------|---------------------|
| OVL-PBG-001 | module.manifest.json slug match | `modules/MMM/module.manifest.json` slug = "MMM" |
| OVL-PBG-002 | BUILD_PROGRESS_TRACKER identity consistent | Module name/slug in tracker matches manifest |
| OVL-PBG-003 | Architecture doc correct module name | Architecture doc references "MMM" (no legacy names) |
| OVL-PBG-004 | IAA Pre-Brief exists before builder delegation | **THIS artifact** satisfies OVL-PBG-004 |
| OVL-PBG-005 | AGENT_HANDOVER_AUTOMATION version cited correctly | Any references match CANON_INVENTORY |
| OVL-PBG-006 | BUILD_PROGRESS_TRACKER uses full 12-stage model | All 12 stages present including Stage 2 (UX Workflow & Wiring Spec) |
| OVL-PBG-007 | Architecture doc references full lifecycle sequence | All 12 stages listed |
| OVL-PBG-008 | Stage gating respected | Prior stages COMPLETE or CS2 exception documented |
| OVL-PBG-009 | Legacy directory numbering advisory | Flag if old directory numbers out of sync |

> **Note on OVL-PBG-006/007**: IAA will check these as part of the full PRE_BUILD_GATES sweep,
> even though the PR only modifies Section 39B. These are mandatory existence checks on the
> module's pre-build governance health. If they reveal gaps, the producing agent must resolve
> them before IAA can issue ASSURANCE-TOKEN.

## Scope Declaration (Hard Boundaries)

**IN SCOPE** — builder MUST deliver:

1. `modules/MMM/00-app-description/MMM_app_description.md`
   - Section 39B: change "FRS derives functional requirements from the App Description" →
     "FRS derives functional requirements from the App Description and the UX Workflow & Wiring Spec (Stage 2)"
   - Version header: bump v0.3.0 → v0.4.0 with Last Updated: 2026-04-07

**OUT OF SCOPE** — builder MUST NOT touch:

- Section 39A (Build Lifecycle Stages) — already reconciled in wave-1266
- Any other section of `MMM_app_description.md`
- `modules/MMM/module.manifest.json`
- `modules/MMM/BUILD_PROGRESS_TRACKER.md`
- `modules/MMM/01-frs/` or any FRS files
- `modules/MMM/02-architecture/` or any architecture files
- `.github/agents/` — no agent contract modifications permitted
- `governance/` — no canon or governance file modifications permitted
- Any application source code, schema, or CI/workflow files

---

## Scope Blockers

> A scope blocker is a condition that would cause IAA to issue REJECTION-PACKAGE
> regardless of the quality of the targeted change.

| Blocker ID | Condition | Action Required |
|-----------|-----------|----------------|
| SB-001 | Builder touches files outside the declared IN SCOPE list | REJECTION-PACKAGE — revert out-of-scope changes |
| SB-002 | Version bump is to a value other than v0.4.0 (e.g., v0.3.1 or v0.5.0) | REJECTION-PACKAGE — must be exactly v0.4.0 |
| SB-003 | Section 39B change does not include "(Stage 2)" qualifier on UX Workflow & Wiring Spec | REJECTION-PACKAGE — stage number is required for traceability to PRE_BUILD_STAGE_MODEL_CANON |
| SB-004 | PREHANDOVER proof missing or not committed before IAA invocation | REJECTION-PACKAGE (CORE-018) |
| SB-005 | `iaa_audit_token` field in PREHANDOVER proof is blank, TBD, or PENDING | REJECTION-PACKAGE (CORE-018c) |
| SB-006 | Session memory from producing agent absent | REJECTION-PACKAGE (CORE-015, CORE-018b) |
| SB-007 | OVL-PBG-006 — BUILD_PROGRESS_TRACKER does not list Stage 2 (UX Workflow & Wiring Spec) | REJECTION-PACKAGE unless resolved by producing agent |

## Required Phases (Final Assurance Invocation)

When the producing agent completes the work and CodexAdvisor invokes IAA for the final
assurance verdict, IAA will execute:

1. **Phase 1** — Identity & Preflight (always)
2. **Phase 2** — Alignment (PR category = PRE_BUILD_STAGE_MODEL, load PRE_BUILD_GATES overlay)
3. **Phase 3** — Assurance Work (all core invariants CORE-001 through CORE-023 + PRE_BUILD_GATES OVL-PBG-001 through OVL-PBG-009)
4. **Phase 4** — Merge Gate Parity, Verdict & Handover (§4.3b token architecture)

**FAIL-ONLY-ONCE rules applied at Phase 3**:
- A-001: IAA invocation evidence present
- A-002: No class exceptions
- A-033: Git-committed verification (all artifacts verified via `git ls-tree HEAD`)
- A-037: Token file `PHASE_B_BLOCKING_TOKEN` field present and non-empty

---

## Required Evidence Artifacts

Before IAA issues the final ASSURANCE-TOKEN, the following artifacts MUST be present and
verifiable via `git ls-tree HEAD`:

| # | Artifact | Required By | Notes |
|---|----------|------------|-------|
| 1 | PREHANDOVER proof from producing agent | CORE-013, CORE-018, A-001 | Must be committed before IAA invocation; read-only post-commit (§4.3b) |
| 2 | `iaa_audit_token` field in PREHANDOVER proof | A-029, A-037 | Pre-populated with expected reference format: `IAA-session-1277-mmm-39b-20260407-PASS` |
| 3 | `## Ripple/Cross-Agent Assessment` section in PREHANDOVER | A-023 | Must explicitly declare NO DOWNSTREAM RIPPLE or list affected files/agents |
| 4 | Session memory from producing agent | CORE-018b | Standard session output |
| 5 | This Pre-Brief artifact | OVL-PBG-004 | Committed before builder work; present on branch |

## PREHANDOVER Proof Required Structure

The producing agent's PREHANDOVER proof MUST include the following sections and fields.
IAA will fail any PREHANDOVER proof that is missing a required field (CORE-018, CORE-020):

```markdown
# [producing-agent] PREHANDOVER Proof — Session [NNN] / Wave mmm-39b-frs-derivation-fix

## Metadata
- session_id: [NNN]
- wave: mmm-39b-frs-derivation-fix
- issue: maturion-isms#1277
- parent_issue: maturion-isms#1266
- branch: [branch name]
- date: 2026-04-07
- producing_agent: [agent name]
- invoking_agent: CodexAdvisor-agent
- iaa_audit_token: IAA-session-1277-mmm-39b-20260407-PASS

## Scope Declaration
[Confirm: ONLY Section 39B and version header modified]

## Artifacts Delivered
- [ ] modules/MMM/00-app-description/MMM_app_description.md (Section 39B + version header only)
- [ ] Session memory file: .agent-workspace/[agent]/memory/session-[NNN]-[date].md

## Change Evidence
- Section 39B before: "FRS derives functional requirements from the App Description"
- Section 39B after:  "FRS derives functional requirements from the App Description and the
  UX Workflow & Wiring Spec (Stage 2)"
- Version before: v0.3.0
- Version after:  v0.4.0
- Last Updated: 2026-04-07

## Ripple/Cross-Agent Assessment
[REQUIRED — A-023 MANDATORY SECTION]
Declare one of:
  NO DOWNSTREAM RIPPLE REQUIRED — justification: [...]
  OR
  DOWNSTREAM RIPPLE REQUIRED — affected files: [...] affected agents: [...]

## Scope Compliance Declaration
Confirm no OUT OF SCOPE files were modified.
Confirm no production code, schema, CI/workflow files were touched.

## CS2 Authorization
Issue #1277 opened by @APGI-cmy (CS2). Assigned to Copilot.
Parent issue #1266 — MMM build lifecycle reconciliation.
```

---

## Ripple Assessment (Pre-Brief Opinion)

> IAA's pre-brief assessment only. Final determination at assurance invocation.

This change corrects a factual omission in the FRS derivation chain. It:
- **Aligns with** `PRE_BUILD_STAGE_MODEL_CANON.md` §Stage 3 (FRS): "Convert the approved
  App Description **and UX Workflow & Wiring Spec** into explicit, traceable functional requirements"
- **Does NOT advance** any module lifecycle stage (this is a correction, not progression)
- **Does NOT trigger** a downstream ripple to `01-frs/` at this point (Section 39B is the
  App Description's statement of derivation intent — the FRS itself is a separate artifact)

**Expected ripple verdict from producing agent**: NO DOWNSTREAM RIPPLE REQUIRED (with justification
that 39B is a governance statement in the App Description, not a change to the FRS content itself)

However: **if the MMM FRS already exists and contains the old derivation language**, the producing
agent must flag this as a scope note for Foreman. That would be a separate wave, not part of this PR.
